---
title: "Visualization"
author: "Bailey Binando, Lillian Pates"
format: html
editor: visual
---

## Quarto

Quarto enables you to weave together content and executable code into a finished document. To learn more about Quarto see <https://quarto.org>.

## Running Code

When you click the **Render** button a document will be generated that includes both content and the output of embedded code. You can embed code like this:

```{r package_load}
#| echo: false

# load packages
library(ggplot2)
library(sf) # vector data
library(terra) # rastor data
library(plotly)
```

You can add options to executable code like this

```{r data_load}
#| echo: false

# load data

# floodplain shapefiles (2yr, 5yr, 10yr, 25yr, 50yr, 100yr)
floodplain_2yr <- st_read("../data/raw/floodplains_HAND/floodplain_HAND_2yr.shp", quiet = TRUE)
floodplain_5yr <- st_read("../data/raw/floodplains_HAND/floodplain_HAND_5yr.shp", quiet = TRUE)
floodplain_10yr <- st_read("../data/raw/floodplains_HAND/floodplain_HAND_10yr.shp", quiet = TRUE)
floodplain_25yr <- st_read("../data/raw/floodplains_HAND/floodplain_HAND_25yr.shp", quiet = TRUE)
floodplain_50yr <- st_read("../data/raw/floodplains_HAND/floodplain_HAND_50yr.shp", quiet = TRUE)
floodplain_100yr <- st_read("../data/raw/floodplains_HAND/floodplain_HAND_100yr.shp", quiet = TRUE)

# poza rica building footprint
buildings <- st_read("../data/raw/microsoft_buildings_pozarica.gpkg", quiet = TRUE)

# dem
dem <- rast("../data/raw/floodplains_HAND/dem_input.tif")

# river
river <- st_read("../data/raw/cazones/rio_cazones_osm.shp", quiet = TRUE)

```

The `echo: false` option disables the printing of code (only output is displayed).

```{r}

library(ggplot2)
library(plotly)
library(viridis)

cols <- viridis(5, option = "plasma", direction = -1)
xmin <- -97.52999
ymin <-  20.43726
xmax <- -97.33529
ymax <-  20.60058

map <- ggplot() +
  geom_sf(data = floodplain_100yr, aes(fill = "Floodplain (100yr)"), alpha = 0.3, color = "black") +
  geom_sf(data = floodplain_50yr, aes(fill = "Floodplain (50yr)"), alpha = 0.1, color = "black") +
  geom_sf(data = floodplain_25yr, aes(fill = "Floodplain (25yr)"), alpha = 0.1, color = "black") +
  geom_sf(data = floodplain_10yr, aes(fill = "Floodplain (10yr)"), alpha = 0.1, color = "black") +
  geom_sf(data = floodplain_5yr, aes(fill = "Floodplain (5yr)"), alpha = 0.1, color = "black") +
  geom_sf(data = buildings, color = "grey60", alpha = 0.75) +
  geom_sf(data = river, color = "darkblue", linewidth = 1) +

  labs(
    title = "Map of Poza Rica, Veracruz, México",
    subtitle = "Words",
    fill = "Flood Extent"
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = c(
      "Floodplain (5yr)" = cols[1],
      "Floodplain (10yr)" = cols[2],
      "Floodplain (25yr)" = cols[3],
      "Floodplain (50yr)" = cols[4],
      "Floodplain (100yr)" = cols[5]
    )
  ) +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(panel.grid = element_blank())

ggplotly(map)


```

```{r}
#| fig-width: 9
#| fig-height: 7
library(ggplot2)
library(plotly)
library(viridis)
library(sf)


#bbox <- st_bbox(buildings)
#bbox
#pad_x <- 
#pad_x <- (bbox["xmax"] - bbox["xmin"]) * 0.03
#pad_y <- (bbox["ymax"] - bbox["ymin"]) * 0.03
  
bbox <- st_bbox(buildings)

xmid <- (bbox["xmin"] + bbox["xmax"]) / 2 #- 0.03
ymid <- (bbox["ymin"] + bbox["ymax"]) / 2

zoom_factor <- 0.35   # smaller = more zoomed in

x_half <- (bbox["xmax"] - bbox["xmin"]) * zoom_factor / 2
y_half <- (bbox["ymax"] - bbox["ymin"]) * zoom_factor / 2


# -----------------------------
# Floodplain colors
# -----------------------------
#cols <- viridis(5, option = "mako", direction = -1)
library(RColorBrewer)
cols <- c("#084594", "#2171b5", "#4292c6", "#6baed6", "#9ecae1", "#c6dbef")

# -----------------------------
# Build ggplot map
# -----------------------------
map <- ggplot() +
  geom_sf(
    data = floodplain_100yr,
    aes(fill = "Floodplain (100yr)"),
    alpha = 0.25,
    #color = "black"
  ) +
  geom_sf(
    data = floodplain_50yr,
    aes(fill = "Floodplain (50yr)"),
    alpha = 0.35,
    #color = "black"
  ) +
  geom_sf(
    data = floodplain_25yr,
    aes(fill = "Floodplain (25yr)"),
    alpha = 0.35,
    #color = "black"
  ) +
  geom_sf(
    data = floodplain_10yr,
    aes(fill = "Floodplain (10yr)"),
    alpha = 0.25,
    #color = "black"
  ) +
  geom_sf(
    data = floodplain_5yr,
    aes(fill = "Floodplain (5yr)"),
    alpha = 0.25,
    #color = "black"
  ) +
  geom_sf(
    data = buildings,
    fill = "grey75",
    color = "grey60",
    alpha = 0.75
  ) +
  geom_sf(
    data = river,
    color = "darkblue",
    linewidth = 1
  ) +
  coord_sf(
  xlim = c(xmid - x_half, xmid + x_half),
  ylim = c(ymid - y_half, ymid + y_half),
  expand = FALSE
) +
  labs(
    title = "Map of Poza Rica, Veracruz, México",
    subtitle = "Interactive floodplain layers",
    fill = "Flood Extent"
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = c(
      "Floodplain (5yr)"   = cols[5],
      "Floodplain (10yr)"  = cols[4],
      "Floodplain (25yr)"  = cols[3],
      "Floodplain (50yr)"  = cols[2],
      "Floodplain (100yr)" = cols[1]
    ),
    breaks = c(
      "Floodplain (5yr)",
      "Floodplain (10yr)",
      "Floodplain (25yr)",
      "Floodplain (50yr)",
      "Floodplain (100yr)"
    )
  ) +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(
    panel.grid = element_blank(),
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5)
  )

# -----------------------------
# Convert to plotly
# -----------------------------
g <- ggplotly(map)

# Hide floodplains initially
for (i in seq_along(g$x$data)) {
  nm <- g$x$data[[i]]$name
  if (!is.null(nm) && grepl("Floodplain", nm)) {
    g$x$data[[i]]$visible <- "legendonly"
  }
}

g



```

```{r}


municipality <- st_read("~/Desktop/ECNS 460/GitHub/ECNS-460/ECNS-460-Project-Binando-Pates/data/raw/mex_admin_boundaries.shp/mex_admin2.shp")

library(dplyr)
library(stringr)

poza_rica <- municipality %>%
  filter(str_detect(adm2_name, "Poza Rica"))

plot(st_geometry(poza_rica))
```

```{r}
library(sf)

library(sf)
library(dplyr)

# make sure CRS matches first
municipality <- st_transform(municipality, st_crs(buildings))

poza_rica <- municipality %>%
  filter(grepl("Poza Rica", adm2_name, ignore.case = TRUE))

# 1) are the geometries valid?
st_is_valid(poza_rica)
table(st_is_valid(buildings))

building_reasons <- st_is_valid(buildings, reason = TRUE)

table(building_reasons[!st_is_valid(buildings)])

library(sf)
library(dplyr)

# match CRS
municipality <- st_transform(municipality, st_crs(buildings))

# get Poza Rica
poza_rica <- municipality %>%
  filter(grepl("Poza Rica", adm2_name, ignore.case = TRUE))

# repair building geometries
buildings_fixed <- st_make_valid(buildings)

# confirm repair worked
table(st_is_valid(buildings_fixed))

buildings_clipped <- buildings_fixed[
  st_intersects(buildings_fixed, poza_rica, sparse = FALSE)[,1],
]

plot(st_geometry(poza_rica), border = "red", lwd = 2)
plot(st_geometry(buildings_clipped), col = "lightblue", border = NA)

```
